alternatives_considered: Path A (fork+rebrand): 2-3d, gives day-one runtime, but creates TS codebase maintenance burden + upstream drift
Path C (port to Go): 2-3wk+, single-binary Go stack, but massive scope creep away from memory layer thesis
Path D (hybrid): adapter now + community port later, but overly complex decision tree
content.context: User asked about replicating openclaude (github.com/Gitlawb/openclaude, 22.5k stars, MIT, TS/Bun) functionality inside MOM CLI. Recon revealed openclaude already solves air-gapped (Ollama/LM Studio) and model-swap (multi-provider). Four paths evaluated: (A) fork+rebrand 2-3d, (B) adapter+upstream PR 3-5d, (C) port to Go 2-3wk, (D) hybrid.
content.decision: Path B — write adapter + submit upstream PR to openclaude (and cline). Do NOT fork, do NOT port to Go, do NOT build in-house MOM Runtime.
impact: In-house MOM Runtime is unnecessary — removed from roadmap consideration
12-24 months of eng freed for memory layer + Scribe + Dashboard
Outreach strategy: PRs to openclaude + cline as awareness vehicle
MCP server becomes the integration surface instead of per-runtime adapters for recall
content.why: OpenClaude IS the MOM Runtime we imagined — provider-agnostic, local-capable, open-source, 22.5k community. Building our own wastes 12-24 months of eng on a solved problem. MOM's value is the memory layer, not the runtime. Submitting PRs to openclaude+cline uses their community size for MOM awareness at near-zero cost.
